US gov't to audit crypto holdings after creating a Bitcoin reserve funded by legal forfeitures. Gov't once held 400,000 Bitcoin after seizing from Silk Road.
news.summary: The US government is gearing up for a comprehensive evaluation of its cryptocurrency assets after establishing a strategic Bitcoin reserve and another collection of digital assets. A top White House official shared this information before a critical industry summit, stating that the review will offer insight into the extent of the government's cryptocurrency holdings. The Bitcoin reserve will be financed using bitcoin accrued through lawful forfeitures, rather than taxpayer money. Additionally, a separate collection of digital assets confiscated through similar processes will be maintained. While the Bitcoin reserve may see growth in the future, the stockpile of other digital currencies will remain static and won't be actively increased beyond current forfeitures. The official reported that the government previously owned around 400,000 Bitcoin, primarily gained after shutting down Silk Road, a prominent illegal online marketplace. Approximately half of the...

news.headline: Inventor from Laughlintown, PA creates Bluetooth stethoscope system, allowing wireless heart & lung monitoring.
news.summary: A nurse from Laughlintown, Pennsylvania expressed her motivation to develop a wireless stethoscope system for listening to heart and lungs. The inventor introduced the BLUETOOTH STETHOSCOPE, aiming to enhance medical monitoring. This innovation could offer significant advantages to healthcare professionals, providing a more convenient and effective way to assess patients' heart and lung functions without the constraints of traditional stethoscopes. The BLUETOOTH STETHOSCOPE could potentially revolutionize medical diagnostics, with its wireless capabilities catering to improved patient care.

US home energy management market report for 2024 shows opportunities for industry players, rising energy costs, and consumer interest in better managing energy consumption. Utilities and providers seek engagement in demand response programs. Smart home and energy devices play a significant role.
news.summary: A new report titled "Home Energy Management: Generation, Consumption, Demand Response" has recently been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. With increasing energy costs and growing concerns regarding grid reliability, consumers are showing a heightened interest in effectively managing their energy usage. Utilities and energy providers are also aiming to enhance participation in demand response initiatives while exploring novel avenues to offer home-related services. The report delves into the integration of smart home and smart energy devices to address these challenges and opportunities in the energy sector.

news.headline: Afiniti appoints 2 new board members, Anurag Yagnik and Chris Karp, strengthening operations and growth.
news.summary: In New York today, Afiniti, Inc., a worldwide provider of customer experience and artificial intelligence solutions, disclosed the addition of two new board members, Anurag Yagnik and Chris Karp. This move comes as the company aims to bolster its operations and expansion efforts. Anurag Yagnik, a proficient technology executive and board member, brings with him more than 25 years of expertise in transitioning traditional software to cloud-based, software-as-a-service (SaaS) platforms. Mr. Yagnik has a rich background in collaborating with private equity-backed businesses.

Crave Brothers shines at 2025 US Cheese Championship with 9 entries in Top 10, out of 11.
news.summary: Crave Brothers Farmstead Cheese stood out at the 2025 U.S. Championship Cheese Contest held in Waterloo, Wisconsin, by achieving outstanding results. Among their eleven entries, nine secured placements in the Top 10 within their respective categories. This impressive performance highlights their excellence in cheese production, with four entries specifically excelling in the competition. The event took place on March 7, 2025, and showcased the quality and competitiveness of Crave Brothers Farmstead Cheese in the industry.

news.headline: BreachLock wins 2 gold awards for PTaaS and Continuous Penetration Testing in 2025 Cybersecurity Excellence Awards.
news.summary: On March 7, 2025, BreachLock, a renowned company in Continuous Attack Surface Discovery and Penetration Testing, was honored with two prestigious gold awards for PTaaS and Continuous Penetration Testing in the 2025 Cybersecurity Excellence Awards. This recognition highlights BreachLock's exceptional contributions to the cybersecurity field. The company has started the year 2025 with remarkable success and achievements.

Trump on Canada tariffs: We may do today, or on Monday or Tuesday.
Over 50% of U.S. adults get 7+ hours of sleep each night, but struggle with sleep quality.
news.summary: A recent Sleep Index Survey has uncovered that over half of adults in the United States are managing to get at least 7 hours of sleep each night. However, despite meeting recommended sleep duration guidelines, many are still facing difficulties with the quality of their sleep. This revelation comes as clocks are due to change soon. The findings, released by Mattress Firm, a leading mattress specialist in the country, emphasize the ongoing struggle with sleep for a significant portion of the population.

TVA seeks proposals for 100MW battery storage at Kingston Energy Complex in Tennessee. Pre-register at tva.com/KingstonBESSRFP.
news.summary: A Request for Proposals has been released by TVA for the installation of a 100-megawatt battery storage system at the Kingston Energy Complex in Roane County, Tennessee. Developers interested in participating in the bidding process must pre-register at tva.com/KingstonBESSRFP. The bid packet will be issued by TVA to the registered developers.

news.headline: Global digital last-mile platform market to reach USD 21.7 billion by 2031 at 14.5% CAGR, driven by smart logistics investments. Key players include Amazon, FedEx, UPS, DHL, Uber Freight. Recent investments include $40M for Swap, $35M for Relay, and $520M agreement between Symbotic and Walmart.
news.summary: The global digital last-mile platform market was valued at USD 6.4 billion in 2022 and is expected to reach USD 21.7 billion by 2031, with a CAGR of 14.5%. Factors driving this growth include the demand for same-day deliveries, integration of AI and IoT in logistics, and the rise of autonomous delivery solutions. The market includes key players like Amazon Logistics, FedEx, UPS, DHL, Uber Freight, and others. Recent developments in the industry include e-commerce logistics startups like Swap and Relay raising significant funds for enhancing their AI-driven platforms for streamlining logistics operations. Symbotic also entered a $520 million agreement with Walmart to develop an automated delivery system for their stores. These investments and partnerships aim to enhance e-commerce logistics and delivery systems, reflecting the industry's rapid growth and innovation.

Lennar starts sales at Addison Hills in Cedar Hill, TX, offering amenity-rich living at an attainable price.
news.summary: In Cedar Hill, Texas on March 7, 2025, Lennar, a top homebuilder in the country, unveiled the launch of sales for Addison Hills. This new community will feature single-family homes and is situated in a picturesque location with greenbelts, playgrounds, water features, and multi-use areas.

Homebuilder Lennar opens Chaselynd Hills community in Magnolia, DE with spacious single-family floorplans in top-rated school district.
news.summary: In Magnolia, Delaware on March 7, 2025, Lennar, a prominent homebuilder in the country, revealed the launch of Chaselynd Hills. This new-home community is situated in Magnolia's high-ranking Caesar Rodney School District. The development offers sprawling single-family floorplans with finishing touches.

news.headline: Inventor from Phoenix develops HELMET CAM motorcycle helmet to see blind spots when changing lanes.
news.summary: On March 7, 2025, in Pittsburgh, an inventor from Phoenix, Arizona, shared their creation of the HELMET CAM. The inventor aimed to enhance motorcyclist safety by developing a helmet that offers visibility of blind spots while changing lanes or merging into traffic. This innovative helmet design eliminates the necessity for traditional...

news.headline: Tony Lama introduces new Estancia Lace-Up Casual Shoe in 3 colors, merging tradition and innovation for style and comfort.
news.summary: In Fort Worth, Texas, on March 7, 2025, Tony Lama, renowned for its high-quality cowboy boots, unveiled the Estancia Lace-Up Casual Shoe, blending tradition with modernity. This new offering promises versatility, style, and comfort, catering to customers looking for a combination of elegance and functionality.

news.headline: El Mexicano® Brand partners with Mexican National Soccer Teams in the U.S., in a multiyear deal.
news.summary: On March 7, 2025 in Los Angeles, the FMF and SUM revealed a long-term agreement with El Mexicano® Brand, a top supplier of Mexican food items. The partnership brings together El Mexicano® Brand with the soccer entities for a multiyear deal. This collaboration signifies a significant move in the industry, solidifying the brand's presence among fans and stakeholders.

Warren asks for Trump's crypto czar's financial disclosures to ensure no conflicts; wants to see divestments.
news.summary: A Democratic legislator requested that David Sacks release official documents demonstrating that he had completed the necessary divestments to digital currencies, confirming that they did not pose any conflicts of interest.

news.headline: Trump touts manufacturing turnaround.
Michael Saylor highlights Bhutan for mining Bitcoin using hydropower resources, not purchasing from the market. Bhutan's strategy seen as modern wealth approach.
news.summary: Michael Saylor, Chairman of Strategy, recently drew attention to Bhutan as a nation that has been quietly accumulating Bitcoin reserves, contrasting with the U.S. under Trump's executive order. Rather than buying Bitcoin on the market, Bhutan has opted to mine it utilizing its plentiful hydropower resources. Bernard Smith from Al Jazeera views Bhutan's strategy as an innovative method for wealth building in a country where gold holds cultural importance. This adoption of "digital gold" showcases Bhutan's forward-thinking approach to asset security. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: KLDiscovery appoints Andrew Shimek and Chris Stone to its Board of Directors, effective March 4, 2025, enhancing legal tech expertise.
news.summary: KLDiscovery Inc., based in EDEN PRAIRIE, Minn., a prominent global provider of eDiscovery, information governance, and data management technology solutions, has recently appointed Andrew Shimek and Chris Stone to its Board of Directors, starting from March 4, 2025. Andrew Shimek holds over 20 years of leadership expertise in legal technology and eDiscovery, with a background encompassing executive positions at leading industry firms. Shimek has been instrumental in shaping the industry landscape.
